question: When driving at night and facing an oncoming car, your headlights:

question: What is the recommended rule to maintain a safe following distance?

answers.text: Two-second rule.
Three-second rule.
answers.explanation: To maintain a safe following distance and avoid rear-end collisions, use the 'Three-Second Rule.' This involves counting 'one-thousand-one, one-thousand-two, one-thousand-three' when the vehicle ahead passes a fixed point. If you reach the same point before finishing your count, increase your distance.

answers.text: Four-second rule.

explanation: To maintain a safe following distance and avoid rear-end collisions, use the 'Three-Second Rule.' This involves counting 'one-thousand-one, one-thousand-two, one-thousand-three' when the vehicle ahead passes a fixed point. If you reach the same point before finishing your count, increase your distance.
